description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two prominent black metal hoops at the top and bottom. The wood appears weathered and stained, with visible grain and some discoloration, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The top and bottom surfaces are flat, with the top slightly recessed and bordered by the upper metal hoop. The barrel lacks any visible handles or spouts, and there are no labels or markings on its exterior. The metal hoops are riveted, providing structural reinforcement and a rustic appearance. This type of barrel is commonly used for storage or transport of liquids or dry goods in urban, industrial, or outdoor settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with black metal hoops, flat top and bottom, and no visible handles or markings. The wood shows signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: The barrel's wood grain and lighter discoloration are prominent, with the upper and lower black metal hoops clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view shows a darker, more stained section of the wood, with the metal hoops and flat top still visible.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this side displays lighter wood tones and the same structural features, with subtle differences in wood grain.
description_view_3: The wood appears darkest in this view, with the metal hoops and overall cylindrical form remaining consistent.
